sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: RIGHT.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 73, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, SPACE, MOUSE, ACTION7.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Knowledge ledger carried from earlier turns:
Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: {'RIGHT': 'Moves avatar +3 cols right, charcoal moves -3 cols (opposite) (VERIFIED steps 1-4,56)', 'LEFT': 'Moves avatar -3 cols left, charcoal moves +3 cols (opposite) (VERIFIED steps 5-6,58-61)', 'DOWN': 'Moves avatar +3 rows down, charcoal moves -3 rows (opposite) (VERIFIED steps 7-10)', 'UP': 'Moves avatar -3 rows up, charcoal moves +3 rows (opposite) (VERIFIED steps 11-13)', 'SPACE': 'Moves avatar UP 3 rows only in specific conditions (step 51), no effect otherwise (steps 46,53,55)', 'MOUSE': 'No effect (VERIFIED step 43)', 'ACTION7': 'No effect (VERIFIED step 42)'}
- Object taxonomy: {'black_with_dots': 'Player avatar - black shape with 5 white dots, moves with arrow keys by 3 cells', 'charcoal_shape': 'Charcoal object that moves OPPOSITE to avatar by 3 cells', 'yellow_shape': 'Target - yellow L-shape, moves opposite to avatar, grows when avatar approaches (18<->27 pixels)', 'sky_blue_strip': 'Vertical divider at cols 30-32', 'right_border_B': 'Black strip at top-right, can merge with avatar when RIGHT pressed near border', 'yellow_border_strip': 'Yellow strip at col 63, shrinks over time (19->11 pixels)'}
- HUD map: {'bottom_row': 'Black strip at row 63', 'right_col': 'Yellow strip at col 63 - shrinking (possibly timer or progress)'}
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
The `world_model` tool argument is the reliable way to update the ledger. Assistant text is optional; if you write any, keep it short and prefix lines with `Action semantics:`, `Object taxonomy:`, `HUD map:`, `Win pattern:`, `Level log:`, `Cross-level notes:`, `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, or `Failed probes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 72 actions; a strong score needs about 32 or fewer. You are 2.2x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The avatar is now overlapping with the charcoal shape! They're both at the same vertical position (row 15) and the avatar is at col 21, charcoal at col 33. The avatar crossed the divider and is now touching the charcoal.
Looking at the image, the avatar (black with dots) and charcoal (dark gray) are now adjacent/overlapping at the divider. This is a new configuration.
Let me think about what the goal might be:
1. The yellow border strip is growing (62 pixels now, was 19 initially)
2. DOWN caused game over
3. The avatar and charcoal are now adjacent
Maybe the goal is to get the avatar to a specific position relative to the target or charcoal? Or maybe the goal is to avoid game over for a certain number of steps?
Let me try moving UP to see if that does something different. The avatar is at row 15, so moving UP would move it to row 12.
Actually, let me think more carefully. The key pattern I see is:
- Avatar and charcoal move in opposite directions
- Target moves opposite to avatar
- DOWN caused game over
- The yellow border is growing (was shrinking before)
Wait, the yellow border was shrinking before (19->11 pixels) but now it's 62 pixels. This is a significant change. Maybe the game over reset the timer?
Let me try to understand the win condition better. Maybe the goal is to:
1. Get the avatar to the same position as the target
2. Get the avatar and charcoal to overlap
3. Avoid game over while the timer runs out
Since I'm running low on actions and need to find the win condition quickly, let me try a few more exploratory moves. Let me try UP to see if that changes anything significant.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Avatar and charcoal are now adjacent at the divider
# Yellow border is 62 pixels (grew significantly)
# Let me try UP to see if vertical movement changes anything
action(['UP'])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"RIGHT": "Moves avatar +3 cols right, charcoal moves -3 (opposite)", "LEFT": "Moves avatar -3 cols left, charcoal moves +3 (opposite)", "DOWN": "Moves avatar +3 rows down; caused GAME_OVER at step 70", "UP": "Moves avatar -3 rows up, charcoal moves +3 (opposite)", "SPACE": "Moves avatar UP 3 rows only in specific conditions", "MOUSE": "No effect", "ACTION7": "No effect"}, "object_taxonomy": {"black_with_dots": "Player avatar", "charcoal_shape": "Moves OPPOSITE to avatar", "yellow_shape": "Target - moves opposite to avatar", "sky_blue_strip": "Vertical divider at cols 30-32"}, "hud_map": {"right_col": "Yellow strip - timer/progress, now 62 pixels"}, "win_pattern": {}, "level_log": {"level_1": "GAME_OVER triggered by DOWN at step 70, recovered. Avatar and charcoal now adjacent."}, "cross_level_notes": ""}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Avatar at (15,21), charcoal at (15,33), target at (45,51). Yellow border 62 pixels. Game NOT_FINISHED.", "goal_model": "Unknown - trying to find win condition", "recent_findings": ["Avatar and charcoal can be adjacent", "Yellow border grew after game over"], "open_questions": ["What is the win condition?"], "current_plan": "Try UP to explore vertical movement", "strategy_log": [], "failed_probes": ["ACTION7", "MOUSE", "DOWN caused game over"]}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
